nodes.name: Weekly Schedule
nodes.notes: Runs every Monday at 8:00 AM to analyze the previous week's meetings.

nodes.name: Manual Trigger
nodes.notes: Click to run the analysis on-demand.

nodes._comment: FATHOM API v3 FIX: Real endpoint is https://api.fathom.ai/external/v1/meetings, auth is X-Api-Key header (NOT Bearer token). Filter by created_after/created_before with include_transcript=true. Returns items[] with transcript[] array of {speaker, text, timestamp}.
nodes.name: Fathom – Fetch Meetings & Transcripts
nodes.notes: FIXED v3: Uses real Fathom API (api.fathom.ai/external/v1/meetings). Auth = X-Api-Key header. include_transcript=true & include_summary=true returns full transcript[] and default_summary inline. created_after/before filter to the Calendar event's time window. Rate limit: 60 req/min.

nodes.name: Match & Extract Fathom Data
nodes.notes: FIXED v3: Matches Fathom recordings to Calendar events by timestamp proximity (±30 min). Builds readable transcript text from speaker-labeled segments. Extracts per-speaker word counts, Fathom's own action items, and AI summary for enriched prompting.

nodes.name: Prepare AI Prompt
nodes.notes: IMPROVED v3: Prompt now includes Fathom's speaker word counts and pre-detected action items to improve AI accuracy. Passes fathomSummary as additional context. Schema unchanged for DB compatibility.

values.content: You are an expert meeting efficiency analyst. Analyze the provided meeting data and return ONLY a valid JSON object matching the exact schema requested. No markdown. No preamble. No backticks. Pure JSON only. Be precise with time estimates in minutes. All scores are 0-100 integers.

nodes.name: OpenAI – Analyze Meeting
nodes.notes: Uses GPT-4.1 with JSON mode. maxTokens increased to 2000 to handle large participant arrays. temperature=0.1 for consistent scoring.

nodes.name: Compute Scores & Metrics
nodes.notes: FIXED v3: Robust JSON parsing handles all OpenAI output formats. IMPROVED: Merges Fathom-detected action items with AI-detected action items (deduplicating by description). Participant email now stored for better leaderboard identity resolution.

nodes.name: Build Meeting Insert SQL
nodes.notes: Builds raw INSERT SQL with proper escaping for JSONB columns. Bypasses n8n Postgres node's defineBelow JSONB limitation.

nodes.name: Postgres – Upsert Meeting Metrics
nodes.notes: FIXED v5: Uses executeQuery with raw SQL to bypass n8n defineBelow JSONB array limitation. SQL built by upstream Code node with proper escaping.

nodes.name: Explode Participants
nodes.notes: ADDED v3: participant_email now stored for identity resolution across meetings.

nodes.name: Postgres – Upsert Participant Metrics
nodes.notes: FIXED v5: Uses executeQuery with raw SQL. ON CONFLICT (meeting_id, participant_name) DO NOTHING handles re-runs. SQL built by upstream Explode Participants node.

nodes.name: Aggregate Trigger
nodes.notes: Reduces N items to a single trigger so downstream query nodes execute exactly once.

parameters.query: =SELECT meeting_id, summary, start_time, end_time, duration_minutes, meeting_type, is_recurring, is_back_to_back, attendee_count, has_transcript, fathom_call_id, decision_minutes, discussion_minutes, effective_minutes, idle_minutes, decision_discussion_ratio, effective_time_percent, meeting_score, meeting_necessity_score, meeting_necessity_reason, sentiment, key_decisions, action_items, unresolved_items, improvement_suggestions, quality_summary FROM meeting_metrics WHERE start_time >= '{{ $('Resolve Inputs & Defaults').first().json.from }}' AND start_time <= '{{ $('Resolve Inputs & Defaults').first().json.to }}' ORDER BY meeting_score DESC

=SELECT COUNT(*) as prev_count, COALESCE(ROUND(AVG(meeting_score)::numeric, 1), 0) as prev_avg_score, COALESCE(ROUND(AVG(effective_time_percent)::numeric, 1), 0) as prev_avg_effective, COALESCE(ROUND(SUM(duration_minutes)::numeric, 1), 0) as prev_total_minutes, COALESCE(ROUND(AVG(decision_discussion_ratio)::numeric, 1), 0) as prev_avg_ddr, COALESCE(ROUND(AVG(meeting_necessity_score)::numeric, 1), 0) as prev_avg_necessity FROM meeting_metrics WHERE start_time >= '{{ $('Resolve Inputs & Defaults').first().json.prevFrom }}' AND start_time < '{{ $('Resolve Inputs & Defaults').first().json.prevTo }}'

=SELECT COALESCE(participant_email, participant_name) as identity, participant_name, COUNT(DISTINCT meeting_id) as meetings_attended, ROUND(AVG(contribution_score)::numeric, 1) as avg_score, ROUND(SUM(speaking_time_minutes)::numeric, 1) as total_speaking, SUM(decision_contributions) as total_decisions, SUM(discussion_contributions) as total_discussions, MODE() WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY role) as most_common_role FROM participant_metrics WHERE meeting_date >= '{{ $('Resolve Inputs & Defaults').first().json.from }}' AND meeting_date <= '{{ $('Resolve Inputs & Defaults').first().json.to }}' GROUP BY COALESCE(participant_email, participant_name), participant_name ORDER BY avg_score DESC

nodes.name: Query Participant Leaderboard
nodes.notes: FIXED v3: Groups by email OR name to deduplicate same person with different name spellings. Shows most_common_role column.

nodes.name: Build Weekly Report
nodes.notes: UPDATED v3: OrangeHRM brand palette applied (#FF6600 primary, charcoal header, semantic green/red). Rich HTML email with 9 sections.

parameters.formDescription: Configure your analysis. Leave blank to use defaults (last full week, standard work hours).

options.responseText: ✅ Analysis started! Check your email for the report.

nodes.name: Form Trigger – User Input
nodes.notes: User-facing form. All fields optional. If skipped, defaults to last full week + 8h/5d work week. Access via the form URL shown in n8n.

nodes.name: Resolve Inputs & Defaults
nodes.notes: Smart merge of user form inputs with defaults. If user skipped form, falls back to last full week + 8h/5d. Passes minDuration and typeFilter downstream.

nodes.name: Webhook – Dashboard Trigger
nodes.notes: Called by the interactive dashboard's 'Run Analysis' button. Accepts JSON body: {dateFrom, dateTo, workHours, workDays}. Returns immediately via Respond to Webhook node.

nodes.name: Webhook – Serve Dashboard
nodes.notes: GET request serves the live interactive HTML dashboard. Share this URL with your team.

nodes.name: Build Dashboard HTML
nodes.notes: Builds the full interactive HTML dashboard from live DB data. Queries come from Dash – Query Meetings/Participants (webhook path) or from main flow query nodes.

nodes.name: Respond with Dashboard
nodes.notes: Returns the dashboard HTML as a proper HTML response. Open the webhook URL in any browser to see the live dashboard.

parameters.query: SELECT meeting_id, summary, start_time, end_time, duration_minutes, meeting_type, is_recurring, is_back_to_back, attendee_count, has_transcript, fathom_call_id, decision_minutes, discussion_minutes, effective_minutes, idle_minutes, decision_discussion_ratio, effective_time_percent, meeting_score, meeting_necessity_score, meeting_necessity_reason, sentiment, key_decisions, action_items, unresolved_items, improvement_suggestions, quality_summary, created_at FROM meeting_metrics WHERE start_time >= NOW() - INTERVAL '14 days' ORDER BY meeting_score DESC;

nodes.name: Dash – Query Meetings
nodes.notes: Queries the latest 14 days of meeting metrics from DB for the standalone dashboard.

parameters.query: SELECT COALESCE(participant_email, participant_name) as identity, participant_name, COUNT(DISTINCT meeting_id) as meetings_attended, ROUND(AVG(contribution_score)::numeric, 1) as avg_score, ROUND(SUM(speaking_time_minutes)::numeric, 1) as total_speaking, SUM(decision_contributions) as total_decisions, SUM(discussion_contributions) as total_discussions, MODE() WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY role) as most_common_role FROM participant_metrics WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '14 days' GROUP BY COALESCE(participant_email, participant_name), participant_name ORDER BY avg_score DESC;

nodes.name: Dash – Query Participants
nodes.notes: Queries participant leaderboard data for the standalone dashboard.

parameters.completionMessage:
Your meeting analysis is running in the background.

You will receive the full report by email in approximately 2 minutes.

You can also open the live dashboard to see results as they appear.

nodes.name: Form Ending – Confirmation
nodes.notes: Shows a confirmation page to the user after they submit the analysis form. Required by n8n — Form Trigger cannot use Respond to Webhook.

parameters.responseBody: ={"status":"started","message":"Analysis running in background. Check your email in ~2 minutes or open the dashboard."}

nodes.name: Respond to Webhook
nodes.notes: Immediately acknowledges the dashboard POST webhook so the browser UI does not hang. Only connected to Webhook – Dashboard Trigger (NOT Form Trigger).

nodes.name: Build Self Evaluation
nodes.notes: Generates personalized self-evaluation report for the calendar owner. Identifies self as the participant with highest meeting attendance. Includes skill assessment, strengths, improvements, and next-meeting prep guide.

fathomAPIFixes.responseShape: { items: [{title, meeting_title, scheduled_start_time, transcript:[{speaker:{display_name, matched_calendar_invitee_email}, text, timestamp}], default_summary:{markdown_formatted}, action_items:[{description,assignee,completed}], calendar_invitees:[{name,email}]}], next_cursor }
fathomAPIFixes.matchingStrategy: Match Fathom recording to Calendar event by scheduled_start_time proximity (within ±30 min)
fathomAPIFixes.rateLimit: 60 requests/minute per user across all API keys
fathomAPIFixes.apiKeyLocation: Fathom Settings → API Access → Generate API Key

workflowImprovementsFromV2: FIXED: Fathom endpoint, auth header, and response parsing completely rewritten
FIXED: Matching algorithm uses ±30 min time-proximity instead of title string match
FIXED: Transcript extracted as readable [timestamp] Speaker: text format for AI
FIXED: Speaker word counts pre-computed and passed to AI prompt
ADDED: Fathom action items merged with AI-detected action items (deduplicated)
ADDED: Fathom AI summary passed as additional AI context
ADDED: participant_email stored in DB for identity deduplication
ADDED: fathom_call_id stored in DB, linked in email report
ADDED: Fathom coverage stat in report header
IMPROVED: Leaderboard now groups by email OR name to handle name variations
IMPROVED: OpenAI maxTokens increased to 2000 for large meetings
setupSteps: 1. POSTGRES: Run the SQL in the separate setup file (meeting-efficiency-db-setup.sql)
2. GOOGLE CALENDAR: Set up OAuth2 credentials → replace YOUR_GOOGLE_CALENDAR_CREDENTIAL_ID
3. FATHOM: Go to fathom.video/customize#api-access-header → Generate API Key → add as HTTP Header Auth credential in n8n with header name 'X-Api-Key' → replace credential reference in node sma-6
4. OPENAI: Add OpenAI API key as credential → replace YOUR_OPENAI_CREDENTIAL_ID
5. POSTGRES CREDENTIALS: Add Postgres credentials → replace all YOUR_POSTGRES_CREDENTIAL_ID
6. GMAIL: Set up Gmail OAuth2 credentials → replace YOUR_GMAIL_CREDENTIAL_ID → set sendTo email
7. TEST: Use Manual Trigger first to verify each node step-by-step
8. FORM URL: Activate workflow → copy Form Trigger URL → share with team for on-demand analysis
9. DASHBOARD URL: Copy 'Webhook – Serve Dashboard' production URL → bookmark in browser → share with stakeholders
10. WEBHOOK CONFIG: In Build Dashboard HTML node, replace __WEBHOOK_BASE_URL__ with your n8n cloud base URL (e.g. https://your-org.app.n8n.cloud)
v4Changes: ADDED: n8n Form Trigger with 6 optional fields (date range, work hours, min duration, type filter)
ADDED: Resolve Inputs & Defaults node — merges user input with smart defaults (last full week, 8h/5d)
ADDED: Webhook trigger from dashboard — users can trigger analysis directly from the browser UI
ADDED: Live interactive HTML dashboard served via GET webhook — open in any browser
ADDED: Dashboard has form panel to run custom analyses without going into n8n
ADDED: Auto-refresh after 90s when analysis triggered from dashboard
CHANGED: Build Weekly Report now branches to both Gmail AND Build Dashboard HTML
CHANGED: Filter & Classify Events now respects minDuration and typeFilter from user input
DASHBOARD URL: Copy the 'Webhook – Serve Dashboard' production URL and bookmark it
v41Fixes: FIXED: Removed Respond to Webhook from Form Trigger path — incompatible combination
FIXED: Added n8n Form node (Page Type = completionPage) as Form Ending — correct pattern
FIXED: Deleted stale 'Compute Time Window' connection leftover from v3
FIXED: Form Trigger now branches correctly: Form Ending (user sees confirmation) + Resolve Inputs (analysis runs)
FIXED: Webhook – Dashboard Trigger correctly branches: Respond to Webhook (immediate ack) + Resolve Inputs (analysis)
NOTE: Form Trigger version 2.2 → update to 2.5 in n8n UI (right-click node → Update version)
v5Changes: FIXED: Model updated from GPT-4.1 to GPT-4.1 for superior instruction-following and structured output
FIXED: Dashboard webhook path now has its own DB query nodes (Dash – Query Meetings, Dash – Query Participants)
FIXED: Dashboard works independently — loads data directly from PostgreSQL, no need for main flow to have run first
FIXED: Dead reference to non-existent 'Compute Time Window' node replaced with 'Resolve Inputs & Defaults'
FIXED: Build Dashboard HTML uses try/catch fallback for all $() references — crash-proof on any trigger path
FIXED: Build Weekly Report references fixed for renamed node
CHANGED: Dashboard no longer branches from Build Weekly Report — it is a fully independent GET endpoint
CHANGED: Dashboard queries last 14 days of data (not just last 3 hours) for richer historical view
ARCHITECTURE: Two fully independent paths now: (1) Analysis flow: triggers → Calendar → Fathom → AI → DB → Email, (2) Dashboard flow: GET webhook → Query DB → Build HTML → Serve
